originalTitle: Hundreds of captives freed from Boko Haram mountain hideout
title: Hundreds of captives freed from Boko Haram mountain hideout
content: The group includes many women and children who were abducted in March from an area close to Cameroon.

originalTitle: 'It was either killed or be killed' - ongoing nightmares of an ex-child soldier in Somalia
title: Yusuf's Big Story: Hope After Hard Times in Somalia!
content:
Hello, my wonderful friends! I'm your special news reporter from sunny Africa. Today, I want to tell you a very important story. It's about a kind man named Yusuf Ali. Yusuf lives in a country called Somalia. It's a beautiful place with sandy beaches and warm sunshine. But Yusuf has some sad memories from a long time ago. He is 34 years old now, which means he's a grown-up, but when he was a little boy, something very difficult happened to him. We are going to learn about Yusuf and how he is trying to feel better every day. Let's listen closely to his story.

Meet Our Friend, Yusuf Ali

Yusuf Ali is a man who lives in a big city called Mogadishu. Mogadishu is the capital city of Somalia. It is a busy city with lots of people, cars, and markets. Yusuf is 34 years old now. He loves his country and wants to help make it a very good place for everyone. But sometimes, Yusuf feels sad because he remembers bad things from when he was much, much younger. Imagine having a big, heavy backpack full of sad memories. That's a bit like how Yusuf feels sometimes. He is very brave to keep going every day. Did you know? Mogadishu is on the coast of Somalia, right next to the big, blue Indian Ocean! It has amazing beaches.

What is a Child Soldier?

Now, my friends, this is a very important part of Yusuf's story. When Yusuf was a little boy, much younger than 34, he was what we call a "child soldier." What does that mean? It means that grown-up people made him join a fight. They made him carry big guns and do things that children should never, ever do. Children should be playing, going to school, and being safe with their families. They should not be fighting in a war. It is very, very wrong to make children fight. Did you know? There are rules around the world that say children must be protected. No one should ever force a child to fight in a war!

Yusuf's Hard, Hard Choices

When Yusuf was a child soldier, he was in a very scary place. He told people that it felt like he had to choose between being hurt or hurting someone else to stay safe. Imagine you are in a very dark room and you are scared. Yusuf felt like that every day. He was forced to make very hard choices just to survive. It was not his fault. He was just a child trying to stay alive in a very grown-up and dangerous world. He saw many sad things that no child should ever see. Did you know? Being scared for a long time can make your brain remember those feelings very strongly. It's like a video playing in your head.

What Yusuf Remembers Now

Even though Yusuf is a grown-up man today, those scary times are still in his head. Sometimes, he has bad dreams when he sleeps. These dreams are like playing a scary movie from his past. During the day, some things can remind him of the war. Maybe a loud noise, or seeing something that looks familiar to a bad memory. This makes him feel scared or sad all over again. It's like a cloud of sadness follows him sometimes. He lives in Mogadishu, and sometimes the city itself has reminders of the past troubles. Did you know? Memories are like little stories our brains keep. Happy memories make us smile, but sad or scary memories can make us feel worried.

Living in Mogadishu Today

Mogadishu is a busy city, and it is working hard to be peaceful and safe. Lots of children there go to school and play. But sometimes, when Yusuf walks around, he sees buildings that are still broken from the war. Or he hears stories that remind him of his time as a child soldier. These things are like "dark reminders" – they bring back the sad feelings. Yusuf is trying his best to live a happy life now. He wants to leave those sad memories behind, but it is a long and brave journey. Did you know? Somalia is a country in East Africa. It has a very long coastline, which means lots of beautiful beaches!

How Yusuf is Getting Stronger

Yusuf is a very strong man, not just in his muscles, but in his heart and mind too. He is getting help to understand his memories. Some special grown-ups, like doctors and helpers, talk to him. They teach him ways to feel calmer when he is sad or scared. They help him understand that what happened was not his fault. He is learning to be kind to himself. This help is very important. It's like giving his mind a warm hug to heal the sadness inside. Yusuf is trying to build a new, happy life for himself now. Did you know? Talking about your feelings, even when they are sad or scary, can help you feel much better. It's like sharing a heavy load.

Why This is Important for Us

My dear friends, Yusuf's story teaches us something very important. It teaches us that war is a very bad thing, especially for children. Children should always be safe. It also teaches us about kindness and understanding. When someone has gone through something very hard, like Yusuf, they need our kindness. We need to remember that everyone deserves a chance to be happy and safe, no matter what their past was. We can all hope for peace in the world, so no child ever has to be a soldier again. Did you know? Many people around the world work very hard to make sure children are safe and protected. They are like superheroes for kids!

Let's Help and Hope for Peace!

So, what can we do? We can learn about stories like Yusuf's. We can be kind to everyone we meet. We can tell our grown-ups that we want peace in the world. We can also think about how brave Yusuf is. He is trying so hard to heal and live a good life. We send him big smiles and warm thoughts from all of us! Let's hope that one day, all children in Somalia and all over Africa, and indeed, all over the world, can play freely, learn, and be safe, safe, safe. Thank you for listening to Yusuf's special story.
summary: Yusuf Ali, a brave man from Somalia, shares his story of being a child soldier a long time ago and how he is now trying to heal from his sad memories with help and hope for a peaceful future.
whyMatters: This story matters because it teaches us that all children should be safe and happy, not fighting in wars, and that we can be kind and help people like Yusuf heal from hard times.
